subject: Public Health
title: Hygiene Behavior Change
description: A micro-lesson that makes Hygiene Behavior Change usable.

hook.text: Knowing you should wash your hands doesn't mean you will. Knowledge alone changes almost nothing.

buildup.text: Behavior change requires triggers—disgust, social norms, and convenience matter more than information.

discovery.text: Placing soap next to the toilet—not in a separate room—increases handwashing rates dramatically.

twist.text: Nudges—like painted footsteps leading to handwashing stations—work better than health education posters.

climax.text: Effective hygiene programs combine emotional appeals, social influence, and environmental design.

punchline.text: Make the healthy choice the easy choice, and people will take it.

quiz.question: What matters more than information for handwashing behavior change?
options: Triggers like disgust, social norms, and convenience
Financial penalties for not washing hands
